pattern: Pieces never pass through each other, so reachability reduces to checking that the non-blank characters match in order and that each L only moves left while each R only moves right.
hints: Blanks are interchangeable, so first compare the two strings after deleting underscores; if those sequences differ the answer is already no.
An L can only drift left, so its position in the target must not be to the right of its start; symmetrically an R can only drift right.
Match the i-th piece of start to the i-th piece of target by walking both, comparing letters and the direction each one moved.

edge_inputs.description: an R and an L would have to cross each other
multiple pieces all shifting in legal directions
piece counts match but letter order differs

stress.description: random start, then derive a reachable-or-not target by occasionally shuffling blanks legally or planting a violation
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 4000))
    start = ''.join(rng.choice('LR_') for _ in range(n))
    # build target by random independent generation; correctness checked by oracle
    target = ''.join(rng.choice('LR_') for _ in range(n))
    return [start, target]
stress.note: purely random pairs exercise both reachable and unreachable cases
